questions: You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your coworker stays late to help you finish a report before the deadline. How do you respond?
You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your neighbor shovels your driveway without being asked after a big snowstorm. What do you say?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. A mentor spends an hour reviewing your resume and writes you a glowing recommendation letter. How do you react?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your friend drives two hours to pick you up when your car breaks down in the middle of nowhere. What do you say?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. A stranger at the airport notices you left your passport at security and runs to bring it back to you. How do you respond?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your partner takes over all the household chores during a week when you're overwhelmed with work. How do you react?
You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. A professor extends your deadline after hearing about your family emergency. What do you say?
You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your sibling surprises you with tickets to a show you've been wanting to see. How do you respond?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. A former teacher reaches out years later to say how proud they are of what you've accomplished. How do you react?

You're someone who takes things as they come without making a fuss about what others do for you. Your team rallies around you and covers your shifts while you recover from surgery. What do you say when you come back?
